title: Queue Reordering on Patient Addition and Urgency Change
description: The system shall automatically reorder the patient queue when a new patient is added or when an existing patient's urgency level is changed.
rationale: To ensure that the queue always reflects the current priority, allowing nurses to quickly identify and attend to patients based on their urgency levels.
business_value: Improves patient care by ensuring timely treatment of those who require it most urgently. Reduces wait times for critical cases and minimizes delays in patient care.

source.description: As a triage nurse I need the queue to automatically reorder when a new patient is added or when an existing patient's urgency level is changed (re-triage) so that the queue always reflects the current priority.

acceptance_criteria: Given a queue with existing patients sorted by urgency level, When a new patient is added with a specific urgency level, Then the queue reorders to place the new patient in the correct position based on priority.

Given a queue with a patient whose urgency level is 'non-urgent', When the nurse changes that patient's urgency level to 'critical', Then the queue reorders to move that patient to the front of the queue.
preconditions: The system has a functional triage queue.
Patients have an assigned urgency level (critical, urgent, non-urgent).
Nurses have access to update patients' urgency levels.
postconditions: The queue is reordered correctly after a new patient is added or an existing patient's urgency level is changed.
All patients in the queue are visible and accessible by nurses.
No errors occur during the reordering process.
functional_constraints: Reordering must be based on the patient's urgency level only.
Concurrent updates from multiple users must be handled without errors.
security_requirements: Access to update patients' urgency levels is restricted to authorized personnel (nurses).
The system must ensure data integrity and prevent unauthorized access to patient information.

non_functional_requirements.performance: Queue reordering should complete within 2 seconds.
non_functional_requirements.scalability: System should handle up to 100 concurrent users without performance degradation.
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error messages should be clear and actionable.

edge_cases: What happens if the system receives a patient with an urgency level that is not recognized?
How does the system handle concurrent updates when multiple nurses are changing patients' urgency levels simultaneously?
dependencies: The triage queue system must be up and running.
Patients must have an assigned urgency level (critical, urgent, non-urgent).
Nurses need access to update patients' urgency levels.

interfaces.name: Patient API
interfaces.description: The requirement interacts with the Patient API to retrieve and update patient information.

interfaces.name: Queue Database
interfaces.description: The requirement interacts with the Queue Database to store and reorder patient data.

test_cases.description: Adding a new 'critical' urgency level patient should place them at the front of the queue.
test_cases.expected_result: The new critical patient is placed at the front of the queue.

test_cases.description: Changing an existing 'non-urgent' patient to 'critical' urgency level should move them to the front of the queue.
test_cases.expected_result: The existing non-urgent patient is moved to the front of the queue.

test_cases.description: Concurrent updates from multiple nurses changing patients' urgency levels should result in a correct queue order.
test_cases.expected_result: All concurrent changes are applied correctly, and the queue is reordered accordingly.
